lines.english: What kind of fish are they?
lines.raw: What kind of fish are they? | 它们是什么鱼？

lines.english: Fish and chips has always been a favourite dish in Britain,
lines.chinese: 炸鱼薯条一直是英国人最喜欢的菜肴，
lines.raw: Fish and chips has always been a favourite dish in Britain, | 炸鱼薯条一直是英国人最喜欢的菜肴，

lines.english: but as the oceans have been overfished, fish has become more and more expensive.
lines.chinese: 但由于海洋被过度捕捞，鱼类变得越来越昂贵。
lines.raw: but as the oceans have been overfished, fish has become more and more expensive. | 但由于海洋被过度捕捞，鱼类变得越来越昂贵。

lines.english: So it comes as a surprise to learn that giant fish are terrifying the divers on North Sea oil rigs.
lines.chinese: 因此，当得知巨型鱼类正在恐吓北海石油钻井平台的潜水员时，这令人感到惊讶。
lines.raw: So it comes as a surprise to learn that giant fish are terrifying the divers on North Sea oil rigs. | 因此，当得知巨型鱼类正在恐吓北海石油钻井平台的潜水员时，这令人感到惊讶。

lines.english: Oil rigs have to be repaired frequently and divers, who often have to work in darkness a hundred feet under water,
lines.chinese: 石油钻井平台需要经常维修，而潜水员通常必须在水下百英尺的黑暗中工作，
lines.raw: Oil rigs have to be repaired frequently and divers, who often have to work in darkness a hundred feet under water, | 石油钻井平台需要经常维修，而潜水员通常必须在水下百英尺的黑暗中工作，

lines.english: have been frightened out of their wits by giant fish bumping into them as they work.
lines.chinese: 他们在工作时被撞到他们的巨型鱼吓得魂不附体。
lines.raw: have been frightened out of their wits by giant fish bumping into them as they work. | 他们在工作时被撞到他们的巨型鱼吓得魂不附体。

lines.english: Now they have had special cages made to protect them from these monsters.
lines.chinese: 现在他们已经制造了特殊的笼子来保护他们免受这些“怪物”的侵害。
lines.raw: Now they have had special cages made to protect them from these monsters. | 现在他们已经制造了特殊的笼子来保护他们免受这些“怪物”的侵害。

lines.english: The fish are not sharks or killer whales,
lines.chinese: 这些鱼不是鲨鱼也不是虎鲸，
lines.raw: The fish are not sharks or killer whales, | 这些鱼不是鲨鱼也不是虎鲸，

lines.english: but favourite eating varieties like cod and skate which grow to unnatural sizes,
lines.chinese: 而是人们喜欢吃的品种，比如鳕鱼和鳐鱼，它们长得异常大，
lines.raw: but favourite eating varieties like cod and skate which grow to unnatural sizes, | 而是人们喜欢吃的品种，比如鳕鱼和鳐鱼，它们长得异常大，

lines.english: sometimes as much as twelve feet in length.
lines.chinese: 有时甚至长达十二英尺。
lines.raw: sometimes as much as twelve feet in length. | 有时甚至长达十二英尺。

lines.english: Three factors have caused these fish to grow so large:
lines.chinese: 有三个因素导致这些鱼长得如此巨大：
lines.raw: Three factors have caused these fish to grow so large: | 有三个因素导致这些鱼长得如此巨大：

lines.english: the warm water round the hot oil pipes under the sea;
lines.chinese: 海底热油管道周围的温暖水域；
lines.raw: the warm water round the hot oil pipes under the sea; | 海底热油管道周围的温暖水域；

lines.english: the plentiful supply of food thrown overboard by the crews on the rigs;
lines.chinese: 钻井平台船员扔到海里的丰富食物供应；
lines.raw: the plentiful supply of food thrown overboard by the crews on the rigs; | 钻井平台船员扔到海里的丰富食物供应；

lines.english: the total absence of fishing boats around the oil rigs.
lines.chinese: 以及石油钻井平台周围完全没有渔船。
lines.raw: the total absence of fishing boats around the oil rigs. | 以及石油钻井平台周围完全没有渔船。

lines.english: As a result, the fish just eat and eat and grow and grow in the lovely warm water.
lines.chinese: 结果，这些鱼就在这宜人的暖水中不停地吃，不停地长大。
lines.raw: As a result, the fish just eat and eat and grow and grow in the lovely warm water. | 结果，这些鱼就在这宜人的暖水中不停地吃，不停地长大。
Who eats who? | 到底是谁吃了谁？
